text.title: Interface Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the interface-analysis methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (catalog boundaries): Enumerate every boundary touchpoint; assign IF-id.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the catalog boundaries gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (classify): Tag each as system | user | hardware | communication.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the classify gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (specify): Per-interface spec with all required fields.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the specify gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (merge into landscape): Diff + merge into integration landscape register.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the merge into landscape gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (review and sign off): Architecture + security review; commit; tag release.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the review and sign off gate.

rule.statement: If preconditions for interface-analysis do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
